name: Cin-Chili
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Beef chuck, cut into 3/8-inch cubes
2 whole serranos, seeded and cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 3-quart heavy saucepan, heat the cooking oil over medium-high heat. Add the beef and brown on all sides.
2. Reduce the heat to low. Stir in the dark chili powder, garlic, serranos, and remaining ingredients from step 2. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 1.5 hours, stirring occasionally. Remove the serranos with tongs before they fall apart.
3. Add the seasonings from step 3 to the pot. Return the beef mixture to the pot. Stir in the tomato sauce, broth, bouillon, jalapeno powder, onion powder, and water/broth as needed for consistency. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered until thickened, about 20 minutes.
4. Add cumin and taste. Adjust seasoning with salt or other seasonings if desired. Simmer for an additional 10 minutes. Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired. Makes six servings. Sazon is a blend of MSG, cilantro, and achiote made by Goya Foods.
